description_long: This is a large, bulbous ceramic vase with a wide, flared mouth and a rounded body, featuring intricate sculptural decorations attached to its exterior. The surface is adorned with raised miniature architectural elements resembling small houses or shrines, each with detailed roofs and columns, and several small human or figure-like forms positioned around the structures. The body of the vase is decorated with faded red geometric and symbolic motifs, possibly of cultural or ritual significance, set against a pale, off-white background. The sculpted elements are distributed primarily on one side of the vase, creating a visually dynamic and narrative-rich composition. The overall finish is matte and slightly rough, with visible signs of age and wear, suggesting historical or archaeological value. This vase would serve as a striking decorative or ceremonial object in a public or cultural urban setting.
description: A large, off-white ceramic vase with sculpted miniature buildings and figures, decorated with faded red geometric motifs and a wide flared mouth. The surface is matte and shows signs of age, giving it a historical and ornamental appearance.
description_view_0: The vase is seen with two prominent miniature buildings and several small figures attached to the front, with red motifs visible on the body.
description_view_1: This view highlights the majority of the sculpted elements, showing multiple buildings and figures clustered on one side, with the red patterns more visible.
description_view_2: The side view reveals the rounded, undecorated back of the vase, with only a partial view of the sculpted elements extending from the front.
description_view_3: The rear view shows the plain, slightly textured surface of the vase, with minimal decoration and no sculpted elements visible.
